sourceMovie.title: Ghost Ship
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ghost Ship for the same reason it broke through in 2002: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Steve Beck keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ghostland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost Ship clicked for you, Ghostland (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ghost Ship?
faq.answer: Try Ghostland, Inland Empire, The Ring—each shares DNA with Ghost Ship while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ghost Ship?
faq.answer: Ghost Ship (2002) is best described as Horror, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ghost Ship worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
